title: Appointment of First Ex-officio Chairperson and Constitution of Board of Trust of National Trust, 2000
description: Appointment of First Ex-officio Chairperson and Constitution of Board of Trust of National Trust, 2000
text: REGD. NO. D. L.-33004/99 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Sub-section (ii) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 623 ] NEW DELHI, MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 25,2000/ASVINA 3,1922 MINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 22nd September, 2000 S.O. 880(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 3(4) of the National Trust for Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities Act, 1999, it has been decided that Secretary. Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment will act as ex-officio Chairperson of the National Trust till a full time Chairperson is appointed by the Central Government. [F. No. 1-1/2000-NI-II] GAURI CHATTERJI, Jt. Secy. 2597 GI/2000 (1) -- 1 of 4 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] -- 2 of 4 -- 3 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 22nd September. 2000 S.O. 881(E).—In continuation of this Ministry's notification of even number dated 21st September. 2000 and in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 3(4) of the National Trust for Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy. Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities Act, 1999. the Central Government hereby constitutes the National Trust for Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities Board with immediate effect The Board shall consist of the following members :— 1. To be appointed separately, - Chairperson 2. Smt. Merry Barua, - Member Action for Autism, T-370-F, Chiragh Gaon, Third Floor, New Delhi-110017. 3. Smt Sharda Fatehpuria. - Member Director, Manovikas Kendra Rehabilitation and Research Institute for the Handicapped, 482, Madhudah, Plot 1-24, Sector-J, Eastern Metropolitan Bypass, Calcutta-700078. 4. Smt. Rukmini Krishnaswamy, - Member Director, Spastics Society of India, 5th Cross Indira Nagar, 1st Stage, Bangalore-560038. 5. Shri Damodar Tilak, - Member Association of Parents of Children with Mental Handicap, 17, Kesri Apartments, Dilip Gupta Peth, Mumbai-400016. 6. Prof. K. R. Venkatesha, - Member President. Karnataka Parents Association for Mentally Retarded Citizens, AMH Compound. OffHosur Road, Near Kidwai Memorial Hospital. Bangalore-560029. 7. Shri S. C. Pant, - Member U. P. Parents Association, Lucknow. 8. Shri Akhil Paul, - Member Director. Sense International (India), 405, Favourite Residency, Nr. St. Xavier's School, Memnagar, Ahmedabad-380052. 9. Smt. Marlene Kamdar, - Member Saraswati Kendra Learning Centre. The C. P. Ramaswami Aiyar Foundation. 1. EldamsRoad. Alwarpet, Chennai-600018. 10. Dr. (Smt.) Rajam PRS Pillay. - Member Director, Balavikas Institute Opp. Hindustan Latex, Peroorkada, Trivandrum Kerala-595005. 11. Joint Secretary. - Member Ministry of Social Justice and (Ex-officio) Empowerment 12. Joint Secretary. - —do— Department of Women and Child Development, Government of India. 13. Joint Secretary. - — d o - Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India. 14. Joint Secretary, - —do— Ministry of Finance. Government of India. 15. Joint Secretary, - —do— Ministry of Labour, Government of India. 16. Joint Secretary, - —do— Department of Education, Government of India. 17. Joint Secretary, - —do— Ministry of Urban Affairs and Employment Government of India. 18. Joint Secretary, - —do— Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India. 19-21. 3 persons to be nominated from - —do— association of trade, commerce and industry engaged in philanthropic activities (will be appointed later on). 22. Chief Executive Officer - Member- National Trust for Welfare of Secretary Persons with Autism. Cerebral Palsy. Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities (in the rank of JS. Govt. of India). 2. A Member shall hold office for a term of three years from the date of his/her appointment or until his/ her successor shall have been duly appointed, whichever is longer. -- 3 of 4 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] 3. The general superintendence, direction and management of the affairs and business of the Trust shall vest in Board which may exercise all powers and do all acts and things which may be exercised or done by the Trust. 4. The Board may associate with itself, in such manner and for such purpose as may be determined by regulations, any person whose assistance or advice it may desire for carrying out the objects of the Trust. Such persons shall have a right to take part in the discussions relevant to that purpose but shall not have a right to vote at a meeting of the Board and shall not be member for any other purpose. Further that the maximum number of persons so associated shall not exceed eight and so far as possible the persons so associated shall belong to the registered organisations or from the professionals. [F. No. 1-1/2000-NI-II] GAURICHATTERJI, Jt. Secy. Printed by the Manager, Govt, of India Press, Ring Road. Mayapuri, New Delhi-110064 and Published by the Controller of Publications, Delhi-110054. -- 4 of 4 --
